title: Murder by the Clock
description: Murder by the Clock — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Murder by the Clock" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: Table of Contents
memes.description: Complete table of contents with PN references for Murder by the Clock

memes.label: Adrenaline Injection
memes.description: The experimental medical procedure attempted by Dr. Worth to revive Herbert Endicott by injecting adrenaline directly into his cardiac muscles.

memes.label: Cassidy
memes.description: Young Irish-American detective assigned to guard Herbert Endicott's sickroom. Superstitious and terrified of corpses, he provides comic relief while remaining a competent officer.

memes.label: Central Office Jurisdiction
memes.description: The jurisdictional division between the NYPD Central Office Homicide Bureau and local precinct detectives that shaped the scope and resources of the Endicott murder investigation.

memes.label: Characters Index
memes.description: A complete index of all characters appearing in Murder by the Clock, dynamically generated from the wiki's person-type pages.

memes.label: Chief Andrews
memes.description: The homicide chief of the Central Office, a well-built, alert-looking man of fifty who assigns Lieutenant Valcour to investigate Herbert Endicott's death and oversees the early stages of the case.

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Murder by the Clock was published in 1929 by Rufus King ."

memes.label: Discovery of Herbert Endicott's Body
memes.description: The pivotal event in which Lieutenant Valcour discovers Herbert Endicott's body crumpled in the cupboard of his own bedroom.

memes.label: Discovery of Marge Myles's Body
memes.description: Madame Velasquez discovers the body of her stepdaughter Marge Myles, a turning point that reveals a second murder in the case.

memes.label: Dr. Sanford Worth
memes.description: Family physician to Herbert Endicott who treated his heart condition and performed the emergency adrenaline injection that revived him.

memes.label: Dr. Worth's Adrenaline Injection
memes.description: Dr. Sanford Worth performs an emergency cardiac injection of adrenaline to revive the unconscious Herbert Endicott.

memes.label: Dust Disguise
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour's deduction that Marge Myles used dust from the cupboard shelf to disguise her face when surprised by Herbert Endicott, a key investigative insight that helped establish the intruder's identity.

memes.label: Endicott House
memes.description: The New York City residence of Herbert and Mrs. Endicott, where Herbert Endicott's body was discovered.

memes.label: Endicott House Attic
memes.description: The attic of the Endicott House, where Mrs. Endicott concealed evidence and held secret meetings during the investigation.

memes.label: Endicott House Balcony
memes.description: The shallow stone balcony running the width of the rear of the Endicott House, from which the fatal shot that killed Herbert Endicott was fired.

memes.label: Endicott House Garden
memes.description: The formal garden at the rear of the Endicott House, visible from the balcony and attic, where the murder weapon may have been discarded.

memes.label: Events Timeline
memes.description: A chronological index of all major events in Murder by the Clock, from the discovery of Herbert Endicott's body to the final denouement.

relations.label: The Endicott Murder: A Timeline Reconstruction

memes.label: Evidence in the Endicott Case
memes.description: The collection and analysis of physical evidence gathered by Lieutenant Valcour and the Central Office during the investigation into Herbert Endicott's death, including the alphabet-labeled exhibits found in the bathroom and bedroom.

memes.label: Fatal Shooting of Herbert Endicott
memes.description: Herbert Endicott is shot dead by a bullet fired from the balcony outside his room, the novel's central murder.

memes.label: Fear as Murder Method
memes.description: The central murder mechanism in the Endicott case — the deliberate inducement of fatal cardiac arrest through sudden terror or shock, exploiting Herbert Endicott's known heart condition.

memes.label: Officer Hansen
memes.description: A uniformed police officer assigned to the Endicott case, who guards the house and fires the shot that wounds Thomas Hollander.

memes.label: Harry Myles
memes.description: Marge Myles's deceased husband who drowned in a canoe accident two years before the events of the novel, under circumstances that suggest Marge may have murdered him.

memes.label: Herbert Endicott
memes.description: Murder victim found dead in a cupboard of his own home. Husband of Mrs. Endicott.

memes.label: Herbert Endicott's Bedroom
memes.description: The bedroom of Herbert Endicott in the Endicott House, where he was confined as an invalid and where his body was discovered in the cupboard.

memes.label: Herbert Endicott's Heart Condition
memes.description: The pre-existing cardiac condition that made Herbert Endicott vulnerable to death by fright, exploited by his murderer as the method of killing.

memes.label: Thomas Hollander
memes.description: Intimate friend of Herbert Endicott who saved his life during the war. Called to sit with the unconscious victim on the night of the murder.

memes.label: Hollander's Knife Attack on Endicott
memes.description: Thomas Hollander attempts to stab the unconscious Herbert Endicott with a Damascus steel knife, triggering a police shooting.

memes.label: Hollander Shot by Cassidy and Hansen
memes.description: Police officers Cassidy and Hansen shoot Thomas Hollander as he attacks Herbert Endicott with a knife.

memes.label: Hollander's Motive
memes.description: The complex psychological motive behind Thomas Hollander's attempt to murder Herbert Endicott — a crime driven not by malice but by a twisted desire to protect Mrs. Endicott.

relations.label: summon him to the Endicott house

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "This ebook is the product of many hours of hard work by volunteers for Standard Ebooks , and builds on the hard work of other literature lovers made possible by the public domain."

memes.label: Jerry Smith
memes.description: Thomas Hollander's Southern friend and secretary, interviewed by Lieutenant Valcour about Hollander's relationship with Herbert Endicott.

memes.label: Lieutenant Valcour
memes.description: French-Canadian police lieutenant who investigates the murder of Herbert Endicott.

memes.label: Locations Directory
memes.description: A directory of all locations in Murder by the Clock, from the Endicott mansion and its rooms to off-site locations central to the investigation.

memes.label: Madame Velasquez
memes.description: Marge Myles's flamboyant stepmother, who blackmails her stepdaughter and provides key testimony to Lieutenant Valcour.

memes.label: Marge Myles
memes.description: Herbert Endicott's mistress, a former stage performer and swimmer whose disappearance and death form a parallel mystery to the main murder investigation.

memes.label: Marge Myles' Apartment
memes.description: The apartment of Marge Myles on Riverside Drive, where Herbert Endicott claimed he was headed on the night of his death.

memes.label: Medical Examiner's Office
memes.description: The forensic medical authority responsible for examining Herbert Endicott's body, determining cause of death, and conducting the autopsy in consultation with Dr. Worth.

memes.label: Mrs. Herbert Endicott
memes.description: Wife of Herbert Endicott who discovers his body and initiates the police investigation.

memes.label: Mrs. Endicott Calls the Police
memes.description: Mrs. Endicott's telephone call to police headquarters, dialling Spring 3100, which brings Lieutenant Valcour to the Endicott house.

memes.label: Mrs. Endicott Confronts Valcour
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour presents his case against Mrs. Endicott in the attic confrontation at 6:30 a.m., accusing her of either murdering her husband or harboring the real killer Marge Myles.

memes.label: Mrs. Endicott Disappears
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour discovers that Mrs. Endicott has vanished from her bedroom during the investigation.

memes.label: Mrs. Siddons
memes.description: The housekeeper of the Endicott household, a severe and sharp-tongued woman who harbors deep resentment toward Herbert Endicott.

memes.label: New York Police Department
memes.description: The law enforcement agency responsible for investigating the murder of Herbert Endicott, operating through precinct stations, the Central Office, and the Homicide Bureau.

memes.label: Nurse Murrow
memes.description: The night nurse assigned to watch over the unconscious Herbert Endicott at Dr. Sanford Worth's direction.

memes.label: Nurse Murrow's Night Watch
memes.description: Nurse Murrow's overnight vigil watching over the unconscious Herbert Endicott, during which she interacted with the police guard and witnessed the tense atmosphere of the house under investigation.

memes.label: Nurse Vickers
memes.description: The nurse assigned by Dr. Sanford Worth to stay with Mrs. Endicott overnight after Herbert Endicott's collapse.

memes.label: Officer O'Brian
memes.description: A uniformed police officer stationed at the front door of the Endicott house during the murder investigation.

memes.label: Poison Suspicion
memes.description: The initial hypothesis that Herbert Endicott might have been killed by poison, which was ruled out by autopsy and redirected the investigation toward the unprecedented theory of fear-induced cardiac arrest.

memes.label: Police Deployment at Endicott House
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour seals the Endicott residence and deploys police personnel following the discovery of Herbert Endicott's body.

memes.label: Police Homicide Procedure
memes.description: The formal protocol followed by the New York police when investigating a suspicious death, as demonstrated in the Endicott case.

memes.label: Roberts
memes.description: Mrs. Endicott's personal maid of several years. An Englishwoman privy to the Endicotts' private quarrels, she holds crucial knowledge about the household dynamics.

memes.label: The Clock Motif
memes.description: The recurring motif of clocks and time in Rufus King's murder mystery, where the precise hour of death and its alibi implications drive the investigation.

memes.label: The Clock Motif in Murder by the Clock
memes.description: An analysis of the recurring clock and time imagery in Murder by the Clock, and its relationship to the novel's structure, themes, and title.

memes.label: The Damascus Knife
memes.description: The Damascus steel knife carried by Thomas Hollander — described as a "sticker" used for a paper knife — that became the weapon in his attempted murder of Herbert Endicott.

memes.label: The Endicott Fortune
memes.description: The substantial wealth of the Endicott family, which shaped the social dynamics of the household and provided a potential financial motive for murder.

memes.label: The Endicott Household Staff
memes.description: The domestic staff of the Endicott household — maids, nurses, and other servants whose testimony and actions played critical roles in Lieutenant Valcour's investigation of Herbert Endicott's murder.

memes.label: The Endicott Murder: A Timeline Reconstruction
memes.description: A narrative reconstruction of the critical hours during which Herbert Endicott was murdered, pieced together from Lieutenant Valcour's investigation.

memes.label: The Missing Narcotic
memes.description: Mrs. Endicott pretended to take Dr. Worth's narcotic but secretly poured it out, faking unconsciousness to establish an alibi while the murder of her husband was carried out.

memes.label: The Postscript Forgery
memes.description: The forged postscript added by Mrs. Endicott to a letter from Harry Myles, which transformed an innocent correspondence into false evidence of murderous intent and became the central blackmail document in the Endicott case.

memes.label: The Threatening Note
memes.description: A crude, hand-printed note reading "Thursday 9 p.m." found on Herbert Endicott's desk, which Mrs. Endicott interprets as a death threat.

relations.label: Mrs. Endicott's call to the police

memes.label: "Titlepage"
memes.description: "By Rufus King ."

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "May you do good and not evil."

memes.label: Valcour's Final Denouement
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour unravels the case in the final chapters, revealing the true murderer and motive in a dramatic confrontation.

memes.label: Valcour Interviews Mrs. Siddons
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour's revealing interview with the Endicott housekeeper, Mrs. Siddons, who speaks with startling candor about her employer.

memes.label: Valcour Interviews Roberts
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour conducts a late-night interview with Roberts, the Endicott household secretary, seeking information about Herbert Endicott's condition and the household dynamics.

memes.label: Valcour Summons Hollander
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour telephones Thomas Hollander and summons him to the Endicott house to sit with the unconscious Herbert Endicott, initiating Hollander's direct involvement in the case.

memes.label: Valcour Visits Marge Myles's Apartment
memes.description: Lieutenant Valcour visits Marge Myles's apartment on the Drive, where he encounters her flamboyant stepmother Madame Velasquez.

memes.label: Valcour's Investigative Method
memes.description: An analysis of Lieutenant Valcour's distinctive investigative methodology — a combination of psychological insight, conversational interrogation, meticulous physical observation, and strategic use of limited resources.
